title: Warm up
content: Introductory slide showing three taxi driver perspectives from different Chinese cities: Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou. Each image depicts a driver's view through the car windshield with distinct urban and natural landscapes representing each city's character.
key_claims: Beijing is depicted with modern highways, skyscrapers, and traditional architecture like the Forbidden City
Chongqing is shown with mountainous terrain, cable cars, and stacked traditional buildings
Hangzhou is shown with a lake, greenery, smartphone technology, and AI/robot elements

title: An Evolving Nation
content: Title slide for the presentation, subtitled 'A Ride Through China.' Features images of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architecture including the Forbidden City, illuminated traditional buildings, and a pagoda tower.
key_claims: Presentation theme centers on China's development as viewed through a taxi ride
Visual imagery includes taxi culture and traditional Chinese architecture

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide introduces the first learning session, focusing on investigation and research. It presents a three-step process flow: Local Features leading to Development Strategies, which leads to Outcomes & Positive Impact, illustrated alongside a stylized city map graphic.
key_claims: Session 1 focuses on investigating and researching city development
The process follows three sequential stages: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: An interactive matching exercise slide where students connect three Chinese cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) to their corresponding development strategies. The correct matches are shown via crossed arrows, indicating each city aligns with a different strategy than its position suggests.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with Sustainable urban development
Chongqing is matched with Tourism & Economic growth
Hangzhou is matched with Technology & tradition integration (融合)

title: Session 2 Ask & Analyse: critical analysis & comparative insights
content: This slide outlines task instructions for the second session, dividing students into two role groups: city observers and city experts. City observers select and ask questions to each group, while city experts answer using text-based examples.
key_claims: City observers select 1 question from a list to ask each group
City experts must answer questions using text examples
Session 2 emphasizes critical analysis and comparative insights

title: Session 3 Promote Your City!
content: This slide details a group activity where students act as 'City Promotion Ambassadors' to create a 2-minute promotional speech for their assigned city. Different roles (taxi driver, city experts) have specific content requirements, and speeches must include key phrases and a creative slogan.
key_claims: Each group creates a 2-minute promotional speech as City Promotion Ambassadors
Taxi drivers share a fun story about their city
City experts explain development strategies
Speeches must use at least 3 key phrases from the text and end with a creative slogan

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This summary slide connects the three taxi driver images to their respective city development strategies, reinforcing the concept that China's growth reflects diverse, locally-tailored approaches. It emphasizes the Chinese principle of adapting strategies to local conditions (因地制宜).
key_claims: Beijing's strategy is sustainable urban development
Chongqing's strategy is tourism & economic growth
Hangzhou's strategy is technology & tradition integration
China is an evolving nation with unique development strategies per city
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote development (因地制宜)
